feat: add _skip model variants that train every 4 steps

Add --train-freq flag to rl_server.py to control DQN training frequency.
_skip variants (m0_0_skip, m1_0_skip, m1_1_skip, m2_skip) reuse their
parent model's C mutator and SHM path but train every 4th step, reducing
per-step latency from ~1290us to ~355us. Also removes old unused scripts
and tracked output files, adds run_experiment.sh and benchmark_latency.py.

Fetch full article content from RSS links for deeper LLM analysis

RSS entries now fetch the actual article content from the linked URL
using requests + BeautifulSoup. The LLM receives up to 800 chars of
real article text instead of just the headline, giving it much better
context for intent/urgency/sentiment classification.

- RSSParser.fetch_article_content() extracts readable text from
  <article>, <main>, or <body> tags, stripping scripts/nav/footers
- Context loop fetches articles before LLM extraction
- Display shows link URL and '(full article fetched)' indicator
- Graceful fallback: empty content on timeout/error/JS-only sites
- Added beautifulsoup4 dependency

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf: batch existing-issue lookup to eliminate N+1 gh subprocesses (#178)

Refactor find_existing_issue to accept a pre-fetched slice of issues
instead of spawning a subprocess per finding. file_off_diff_issues now
fetches all conductor-review issues once before the loop using
list_issues_by_search with an empty query (limit 200), then filters
in-memory.

This collapses N search subprocess spawns to 1, eliminating the N+1
problem.

Also add comprehensive unit tests for find_existing_issue covering:
- Exact match
- Case-insensitive substring matching
- No match cases
- Empty issue list

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: make TestIntelligentSelector_UsesFingerprints deterministic

The selector was overriding EpsilonGreedy=0.0 to 0.1, so the routing
test's attempt to disable epsilon-greedy exploration for a deterministic
result silently failed, causing ~10% flaky failures.

Fix by moving the default (0.1) into Config.applyDefaults(), called from
Load(). The selector now uses the config value as-is. Tests that
construct RoutingConfig directly can set EpsilonGreedy=0.0 to genuinely
disable exploration, while production configs loaded via Load() get 0.1
when the field is omitted.

commit.message:
feat(ai): retry LLM calls on malformed structured output JSON (#224)

When using schema-based structured output, LLMs occasionally return
malformed JSON that fails parsing. This adds automatic retry (up to 2
retries) specifically for parse failures, avoiding unnecessary retries
for network or API errors.

* feat: agentic code reasoning + E2E tests + proof harness expansion

Implements Meta's "Agentic Code Reasoning" paper (arxiv 2603.01896) —
structured line-level trace engine that forces evidence for every claim
instead of pattern-matching guesses.

Core module (scripts/code-reasoning.js):
- createTrace/addStep/finalizeTrace primitives with confidence scoring
- traceForSelfHealFix: verifies fix execution, file changes, output
- traceForDpoPair: verifies domain overlap, rubric delta, prompt quality
- traceForProofCheck: verifies individual proof gate assertions
- aggregateTraces: rolls up traces into pass/fail with avg confidence

Integrations:
- self-heal.js: runSelfHeal() returns reasoning + traces per fix
- export-dpo-pairs.js: each DPO pair includes reasoningTrace metadata
- prove-automation.js: 2 new checks (code_reasoning.dpo_traces,
  code_reasoning.proof_gate), report includes reasoning aggregate

Test suite expansion (407 -> 519 tests):
- NEW tests/e2e-pipeline.test.js: 19 cases covering full loop
  (capture -> memory -> DPO export -> prevention rules -> API round-trip)
- prove-adapters.test.js: 1 -> 31 (every check individually asserted)
- prove-automation.test.js: 1 -> 35 (every check individually asserted)
- self-heal.test.js: 2 -> 12
- rubric-engine.test.js: 3 -> 15
- self-healing-check.test.js: 3 -> 10
- NEW tests/code-reasoning.test.js: 22 cases

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: track per-script changed files in self-heal traces

Sentry code review correctly identified that changedFiles was being
attributed to every fix script trace regardless of which script
actually changed the files. Now runFixPlan takes a git diff snapshot
before and after each script, storing per-script changedFiles.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Use @supabase/ssr cookie storage for PKCE code_verifier persistence

Supabase PKCE stores the code_verifier in memory, which is lost between
the authorize and callback serverless invocations. Using @supabase/ssr
with cookie handlers stores the verifier in the browser's cookies via
the redirect response, so it's available when the callback fires.

commit.message:
Fix: Data Agent 404 - avoid Draft-state regression on re-configure

Root cause:
  The Fabric Data Agent /query endpoint returns HTTP 404 EntityNotFound
  even when the Managed Identity IS a workspace member.  The real cause is
  that configure_dataagent() was PATCHing an already-Published agent every
  run, which moves it back to Draft state in the current Fabric preview API.
  The publish endpoints (/publish, /activate, /items/{id}/publish) all return
  HTTP 404, leaving the agent permanently stuck in Draft state — where the
  /query endpoint refuses requests with EntityNotFound.

Changes:

scripts/fabric_setup.py — configure_dataagent():
  - Before PATCH/PUT, call GET /dataAgents/{id} to read current agent state
    and configuration (dataSources list).
  - If the agent is already Published AND the correct Lakehouse is already a
    data source, SKIP the PATCH entirely to avoid the Draft regression.
  - If the agent is in Draft state (already linked), call
    _try_publish_dataagent() without re-patching.
  - If Lakehouse is not yet linked, PATCH as before — then immediately call
    _try_publish_dataagent() to re-publish after the configuration change.
  - Add _try_publish_dataagent() helper that centralises the publish logic
    (PATCH success path now also calls it).

backend/fabric_client.py — chat():
  - Add pre-flight GET /dataAgents/{id} before the first query attempt.
    Logs the agent state (Published/Draft/unknown) so failures show the real
    cause in App Service logs.
  - If state is Draft, log a specific warning with a direct link to the
    Fabric portal to click 'Publish'.
  - Improve the HTTP 404 RuntimeError message: include the raw Fabric error
    body (was hidden before) and list BOTH root causes in priority order:
    (1) agent in Draft state, (2) Managed Identity not in workspace.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(raid): notify Slack channel when Jira close is blocked by unmet prerequisites (#233)

`can_be_closed` already covered all closure prerequisites (wrong status,
PM not linked, PM not ready, missing key events) but the serializer had a
redundant `needs_postmortem` guard that silently returned before reaching
the `can_be_closed` check — preventing the Slack notification from being sent.

Changes:
- Remove redundant `needs_postmortem` block from `_handle_status_update`;
  all cases now fall through to `can_be_closed` which sends the Slack message
- Fix gap in `can_be_closed`: add `POSTMORTEM_NOT_LINKED` reason when no Jira
  PM is linked for a `needs_postmortem` incident (previously could allow closure)
- Fix test DB setup: remove `search_path` from DB OPTIONS in tests via
  `django_db_modify_db_settings` fixture (schema does not exist in fresh test DB)

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
